articles.title: Jang So Jin announces that she is getting married
articles.description: In a heartwarming announcement, Jang So Jin, the former Gugudan star, revealed her upcoming wedding this August. She expressed her happiness in finding her true self with her fiancé, who has enriched her life tremendously. As they embark on this beautiful journey together, she vowed to stand by him, while fans showered them with warm wishes and congratulations across various platforms.

articles.title: Karisma Kapoor on 35 years of debut film Prem Qaidi: ‘I was straight out of school’
articles.description: Karisma Kapoor shares memories of her debut film Prem Qaidi, which released on this day 35 years ago in 1991. She was just 16 when she shot for that movie.

articles.title: Sarah Sherman talks about her love for Paul McCartney
articles.description: In a whimsical moment, Sarah Sherman of Saturday Night Live recounted her encounter with musical legend Paul McCartney. The comedian, sporting her signature mullet, was taken aback when McCartney playfully tugged it, questioning its authenticity. Her admiration for his work, especially the album 'McCartney II,' led to a lighthearted suggestion from him to 'adulate' him—an unforgettable experience for the beloved SNL star.

articles.description: A tragic accident recently took place on the set of Sanjay Leela Bhansali's Love & War, which stars Alia Bhatt, Ranbir Kapoor and Vicky Kaushal in key roles. A worker reportedly died after suffering an electric shock while working on the set.

articles.title: He rejected 'dollar paycheck' in Singapore to live in Bengaluru: Techie walks away from dream job. Is it the right choice?
articles.description: An Indian tech professional, Aman Vaishnav, has gone viral after revealing why he left a high-paying machine learning job in Singapore and returned to India to start over in Bengaluru. In an Instagram video, the 26-year-old said personal circumstances prompted the move and that he chose “the life that feels right” over one that simply looked successful. While admitting he is still unsure whether it was the right decision, Vaishnav said he has never made choices out of fear.

His impeccable delivery not only earned him a standing ovation but also secured him his tenth Golden Star.

articles.description: Yash Toxic: Yash's upcoming film Toxic: A Fairy Tale for Grown-Ups will hit cinemas worldwide on 26 August 2026 after repeated delays. The Geetu Mohandas directorial stars Kiara Advani, Nayanthara and Huma Qureshi in key roles.

articles.title: When Samantha opened up about planning motherhood with Naga Chaitanya
articles.description: Samantha Ruth Prabhu finds herself at the center of new pregnancy speculation following recent festivities. This has drawn attention to her comments from years ago about motherhood and planning for a family with ex-husband Naga Chaitanya, revealing her desire for children as a cornerstone of her life. The film 'Shaakuntalam' was intended as her final project before a family hiatus.

articles.title: Upasana Singh revisits ‘Kaun hai ye aadmi’ moment; Recalls Salman Khan’s reaction; 'we never knew what kind of interaction would happen'
articles.description: Upasana Singh, the beloved "bua" from Comedy Nights With Kapil, revealed the spontaneous origin of her iconic "Kaun Hai Ye Aadmi?" dialogue. The unscripted moment with Salman Khan, initially causing tension on set, unexpectedly became a massive hit. Khan's hearty laughter diffused the situation, leading to the catchphrase becoming a signature element of the show and a pop culture phenomenon. Singh remains open to future television collaborations.

articles.title: Hill Stations Bookings Up 76% In June As Indians Escape Heatwave: Report
articles.description:
New Delhi: As June brought rising temperatures and mid-year travel opportunities across the country, bookings at properties in Shimla surged "76 per cent" year-on-year, reflecting a growing preference among Indians for mountain getaways, according to data released by hospitality and backpacker hostel chain Zostel.

The company also reported nearly "95 per cent growth" in footfall across destinations such as Srinagar, Tirthan Valley and Gangtok compared to the same period last year, highlighting a broader shift towards mountain travel during the month.

Overall mountain bookings across the Zostel network rose between "seven and nine per cent" year-on-year, with nearly "48 per cent" of travellers making their bookings within three days of departure.

The findings also suggested a growing preference for spontaneous travel, with the 72-hour booking window emerging as the dominant booking pattern.

"Mountain bookings across Zostel properties are up by 76 per cent this June compared to last year, and nearly half of those bookings were made within 72hours of the travel date.

"The traditional planning cycle built around early-bird rates and advance consideration is being bypassed by a traveller who has already made up their mind and is comfortable making significant travel decisions on very short notice," Aviral Gupta, CEO of Zostel and Zo world, said in a statement.

The trend was visible beyond Zostel's network as well, with hotels reporting higher occupancy levels during the April-June period.

Hospitality players such as WelcomHeritage Parv Vilas Resort & Spa in Kasauli said occupancy levels typically rise by "15-18 per cent" during these months.

"During this period, hotels generally witness an increase in occupancy levels of approximately 15–18 per cent compared to the rest of the year, primarily driven by summer vacations, family travel, and favorable weather conditions," a spokesperson for the property said.

The Zostel report also highlighted a shift in destination preferences, with travellers increasingly exploring lesser-known mountain locations.

While Shimla remained the most-booked destination, places such as Dobhi, Rajgundha and Kareri recorded some of the strongest footfall growth this season, driven largely by organic demand and peer recommendations.

Alongside these emerging destinations, Srinagar, Tirthan Valley and Gangtok attracted travellers from major cities including Bengaluru and Mumbai, reflecting a growing willingness to travel longer distances for mountain experiences.

The data further points to changing traveller demographics, with non-metro visitors accounting for "67 per cent" of mountain footfall at Zostel properties this June, up from "65 per cent" a year ago.

Among the leading source markets were Bengaluru, Mumbai, Pune, Delhi-NCR and Hyderabad, although the company noted that demand from tier-II and tier-III cities is growing at a faster pace.

(Disclaimer: This report has been published as part of the auto-generated syndicate wire feed. Apart from the headline, no editing has been done in the copy by ABP Live.)

articles.title: Music is the truest form of love I’ve ever experienced: Shruti Haasan
articles.description: “I don’t need a day to celebrate music. On one level, it’s my work. But, on another, it’s my best friend. I always go back to it on a good or a bad day," says Shruti Haasan.

articles.title: Katy Perry playfully calls out exes during concert
articles.description: Katy Perry playfully referenced past relationships during a recent concert, using a stage prop to 'decline' calls from exes like Diplo, John Mayer, and Orlando Bloom. The singer then enthusiastically accepted a call from 'JPJT,' her nod to current partner Justin Trudeau, highlighting their strong bond. This comes after their recent red carpet debut and a romantic picnic, with Perry expressing how Trudeau grounds her.

articles.description: The legal case involving BTS member Jin's fan event kissing incident has taken a fresh turn. A 50-year-old Japanese woman indicted for forcibly kissing the singer has formally objected to the court's trial schedule, raising questions about upcoming proceedings.

articles.title: Cook earning Rs 15,500 left after 4 years despite gifts and salary hikes. Man says her exit taught him a harsh lesson
articles.description: A man's four-year relationship with his cook ended abruptly when she left for a higher-paying job, despite his consistent salary increases, gifts, and bonuses. He learned that perceived loyalty doesn't always equate to shared value, as her decision was driven by a better opportunity. This experience highlighted how individual priorities shape relationships, even those seemingly built on trust and kindness.

The Malayalam thriller has started its box-office run on a strong note, with collections rising significantly on its second day. Positive audience response and word-of-mouth appear to be driving the film's momentum. Read on to know where it will stream online after its theatrical run.

articles.title: Vivek Dahiya reveals his ‘identity’ has changed after becoming a father; says, ‘Dil ki dhadkan badal gayi hai’
articles.description: Actor Vivek Dahiya has opened up about the profound emotional transformation fatherhood has brought into his life. He shared in a recent vlog how his identity as a man has shifted, with his "heartbeat changing" since becoming a parent. Vivek also discussed forming new connections with fellow parents, bonding over conversations about children and schooling, while admitting to constantly missing his babies when apart.

articles.description: Cocktail 2, starring Shahid Kapoor, Kriti Sanon and Rashmika Mandanna, has collected Rs 50.95 crore worldwide within two days of release. The romantic drama witnessed strong growth on Saturday, taking its India net collection to Rs 29.75 crore.

The film, directed by Sasi and starring Vijay Antony, has now amassed Rs 1.97 crore net in India. The story centers on a mother's unwavering wish and her sons' diverging paths, with ETimes praising its departure from conventional village drama tropes.

articles.title: Dilip Kumar had a second marriage with Asma, 16 years after wedding with Saira Banu: 'Can never forgive myself for the hurt I caused her'

Asma later sought privacy in Vancouver, lamenting media intrusion and claiming most reports were false. Dilip Kumar, in his autobiography, admitted the marriage was a mistake orchestrated by his sisters, regretting the pain caused to Saira and their subsequent reconciliation.

articles.title: Not a showstopper act
articles.description: Book review: Vishwas Patil’s Marathi novel on the Indian circus is overburdened by research and detail, weakening narrative flow and character depth. The translation struggles and thin characterisation reduce the impact of an otherwise ambitious historical narrative now

articles.title: Quote of the day by Johnny Depp: 'Growing old is unavoidable, but never growing up is possible. I believe you can retain certain things from your childhood if you protect them...' Hollywood icon teaches an important lesson about staying young at heart
articles.description: Johnny Depp's quote of the day reflects on the difference between growing older and losing the spirit of childhood. The actor suggests that while aging is inevitable, people can preserve qualities such as imagination, curiosity, and wonder if they consciously protect them. The quote is often associated with Depp's views on creativity and his role in Finding Neverland. It also offers insight into the life and career of the Hollywood star, whose unconventional roles and enduring popularity have made him one of cinema's most recognizable figures.

articles.title: Gurmeet Choudhary and Debina Bonnerjee enjoy family time during Jamai Shosthi; see pics
articles.description: Debina Bonnerjee and Gurmeet Choudhary recently observed Jamai Shosthi with their daughters, Lianna and Divisha, showcasing their special family moments. Clad in beautiful traditional outfits, they posted delightful photos of a lovingly prepared feast. Gurmeet reflected on his long-held dream of marrying a Bengali woman, a wish granted with Debina. Their transformation from co-stars to proud parents of two girls was beautifully illustrated during the celebration.
